Description: Use Expo managed workflow for simpler builds and updates; eject only when necessary
Do: Start with managed workflow; use config plugins for native changes; use EAS Build for native builds
Dont: Eject immediately; use bare workflow without reason; manually modify native code

Guideline: Use Expo Router for File-Based Routing
Description: Use expo-router for file-system based navigation with automatic deep linking
Do: Define routes via file structure; use typed routes; leverage automatic deep linking
Dont: Use React Navigation directly; hardcode deep link config; define routes manually

Guideline: Use EAS Build for Cloud Builds
Description: Build iOS and Android binaries in the cloud without local Xcode/Android Studio setup
Do: Use eas build for all builds; configure profiles for dev/preview/production; use EAS Submit for store uploads
Dont: Build locally with Xcode/Android Studio for CI; skip build profiles
Code Bad: # Local builds for CI\nnpx react-native run-ios --configuration Release\n# Requires Mac with Xcode for iOS

Guideline: Use EAS Update for OTA Updates
Description: Push JavaScript bundle updates without going through app store review
Do: Use eas update for JS changes; set update policies; test updates on preview channel first
Dont: Push native changes via OTA; skip testing update on preview; force update without fallback
Code Bad: // Pushing native dependency changes via OTA - will crash\n// No update check - users stuck on old JS bundle\n// No preview channel testing

Guideline: Use Development Builds with Dev Client
Description: Create custom development clients with native modules for testing during development
Do: Use expo-dev-client; create dev builds with EAS; test native modules without ejecting
Dont: Use Expo Go for projects with native modules; rebuild app for every native change
Code Bad: // Using Expo Go with native modules\n// Error: 'expo-camera' requires a custom native module\n// Can't test native features in Expo Go
